design_philosophy.description: A tool that feels like a magical artifact but functions with the precision of a modern dashboard. It prioritizes the user's cognitive load during gameplay over decorative excess.
anti_patterns: No heavy parchment textures that reduce text contrast.
No complex 'Art Deco' borders that eat up screen real estate.
No low-contrast color combinations (e.g., dark blue on black).

themes.name: Aether & Iron
themes.description: A high-contrast, modern magical terminal. Forged in iron, powered by aether. Best for clarity and long sessions.

themes.name: Midnight Forest
themes.description: Deep, organic, and soothing. Like playing D&D in a bioluminescent forest clearing at night.

themes.name: Royal Parchment (Dark)
themes.description: A classic fantasy feel modernized for digital screens. Warm dark greys and browns instead of blinding white paper.

rules: Headings (h1-h3) must use the Theme Heading Font.
Body text, UI labels, and inputs must use the Theme Body Font.
All text size 14px or smaller must have line-height 1.5 or greater.
Never use pure white (#FFFFFF) text on pure black (#000000). Use the theme tokens.

buttons.primary: bg-[var(--role-primary)] text-white hover:brightness-110 shadow-lg transition-all active:scale-95
buttons.secondary: bg-[var(--surface-highlight)] text-[var(--text-primary)] border border-[var(--border)] hover:border-[var(--role-primary)] transition-all

cards.base: bg-[var(--surface)] border border-[var(--border)] rounded-[var(--radius)] shadow-md overflow-hidden

inputs.base: bg-[var(--background)] border border-[var(--border)] rounded-[var(--radius)] px-4 py-2 text-[var(--text-primary)] focus:ring-2 focus:ring-[var(--role-primary)] focus:border-transparent outline-none transition-all

icons.style: Stroke width 1.5px or 2px depending on size.
icons.usage: Use icons to save space. Always include aria-labels.

instructions_to_main_agent: 1. IMPLEMENT THE THEME SWITCHER: Create a ThemeContext that manages the 'currentTheme' state.
2. USE CSS VARIABLES: Define the variables in 'index.css' based on the selected theme. Do not hardcode colors in Tailwind classes (e.g., use 'bg-surface' not 'bg-slate-800').
3. GM VS PLAYER MODE: This is a critical feature. Create a toggle or state 'userMode' (GM | PLAYER). When in GM mode, use the GM accent colors. When in Player mode, use Player accents.
4. CLEANUP: Remove the old 'Art Deco' borders and 'Tron' animations that are too distracting. Replace them with subtle CSS glows/shadows defined in the theme.
5. START with the 'Aether & Iron' theme as default.
6. KEEP IT RESPONSIVE: The GM screen must stack nicely on mobile.
UNIVERSAL_GUIDELINES_FOR_MAIN_AGENT: Dark colors look good when used independently without gradients. DO NOT use dark colors as gradients
Don't make generic centered layouts, simplistic gradients, and uniform styling.
Create depth through layered design elements with z-index hierarchy
Use glass-morphism effects with backdrop filters (12-24px blur) sparingly.
You MUST NOT apply universal transition. Eg: transition: all. This results in breaking transforms. Always add transitions for specific interactive elements like button, input excluding transforms
Every interaction needs micro-animations - hover states, transitions.
Use 2-3x more spacing than feels comfortable. Cramped designs look cheap.
Main agent relies on these image URLs. Missing categories = main agent will use placeholder text which is FORBIDDEN
